lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: Hello there...thanks for helping out Trufitus commune with the gods Bwana. We've been able to return to the village thanks to your help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do here?

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: I's not doing a great deal now Bwana, as I'm no spring chicken. I do my fair share around the village bwana, though, I could tell you a thing or two about the jungle slashing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, tell me about jungle slashing!

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: Ok Bwana, I will! To begin with you'll need a machete in order to hack your way through the jungle. There are some better gem edged weapons which you may be able to purchase from Gabooty.

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: Safta Doc makes them for him. You should be aware that hacking away at the jungle is not without its dangers though Bwana. Many times you may find some scary creatures lurking inside.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What sort of creatures?

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: Ok, to start with there are spiders! These are pretty tough but if you defeat it, you can get a good meal from it, if you know how to cook it that is. My wife cooks them to a turn - Mmmm, delicious.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok go on!

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: Yes Bwana, of course. Then there are snakes...these are dangerous because they're poisonous - so be prepared! However, they're not without their uses. A bush snake is large enough to have a useful hide.

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: Once the hide is tanned you get a snakeskin and you're able to make things from it, the same way that mainlanders use normal leather! However, it's actually still quite small.

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: You may need several skins in order to make something useful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, sounds interesting!

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: Then there are giant mosquitoes bwana, they're fast and agile. You'll need to be quick if you're to hit them at all! However, it certainly does train your reactions. They're not very tough, just difficult to hit.

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: I also believe that their proboscis is useful in someway, I believe Timfraku's sons sometimes use them for hunting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, that sounds helpful...anything else?

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: Yes Bwana, much much more! What next...Oh yes, the tribesmen...you've probably seen them scouting around the village, sometimes sneaking inside the enclosure to steal our chickens!

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: They sometimes hide in the jungle bushes waiting for an opportunity to strike when we least expect it! Take care Bwana, these treacherous jungle natives use poison on their spears!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right Ok, good advice... 'poison on spears....' Anything else I should know about?

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: Yes Bwana... and this is serious... The jungle is very ancient and many unspeakable acts have been witnessed on these lands. Bizarre supernatural curses were placed on criminals and then they were buried alive!

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: These strange creatures have the curse of the Broodoo on them! They'll rise from their grave with their heads in their chests and move like crazy spirits of the afterlife.

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: If they're disturbed they will attack anything in the area with their magical abilities! They suffer little from being attacked as they seem to be protected by some form of defence.

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: However... it is said that the means of their death provides the clue to their destruction. So far three distinct Broodoo have been identified. The Broodoo with a green hued skin is said to have died from poison.

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: There is a broodoo of yellow hue and a pale skinned broodoo, but little is known of these two!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erk! Well that sounds pretty scary!

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: Yes Bwana...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks a lot....

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: Wait Bwana, there's more!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: More... Blimey! Ok carry on...

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: Some of the local villagers have found strange plants which grow here-abouts. If you cut away at the jungle and find one, it's considered a blessing, though the useful part of the plant is usually buried underground.

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: And also, you may find an uncommon rock type which is impregnated with mineral wealth! Well that concludes all the help I can give you regarding the jungle in Tai Bwo Wannai!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have to say, that's quite a lot of information...thanks very much!

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: You're more than welcome bwana!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything interesting to do around here?

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: Bwana, if anyone else asks me that question today, I'll explode! Go and ask someone else!

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: Not that I'm aware of, just sit back and enjoy the sunshine bwana!

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: Sorry Bwana, I'm already busy, why not go and talk to Murcaily! He's around the village somewhere.

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: Well, I think that there's some work to be done...perhaps Murcaily can help you. He usually tends to the hardwood grove to the east of Trufitus's hut.

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: It's just village life as normal around here Bwana, always something interesting to find to occupy your time if you look hard enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been doing some work around the village.

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: Yeah, you've been doing a lot of good work around here. Let me give you something for your time and effort.

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: I've already given you some trading sticks and I don't have any more on me at the moment. Why not go and talk to some of the other villagers and get some contributions?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks.

sections.title: If the player is wearing a tribal mask

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: Ahhhhhhhh - Get away from me you beast!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the problem....oh, I'm wearing this big scary mask! I'd better take it off before I start talking to the natives.

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: Waahhhhh! - It's da evil broodoo beast come ta eat my very soul!

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: Ahhhhhhhh - It's a monster!

lines.speaker: Sharimika
lines.text: Ahhhhhhhh!!
